Now let me check the implementation to see if `executeTuff` already handles signed integers like `"I8"`:

The implementation already supports signed integers (`I` prefix). The regex on line 6 matches `[UI]`, and lines 21-26 handle the signed integer bounds correctly. Let me run the tests to confirm everything passes:

Stderr:

(pass) executeTuff("-100I8") returns -100

Ran 8 tests across 1 file. [52.00ms]
 | Stdout: bun test v1.3.13 (bf2e2cec)

All 8 tests pass, including the new one `executeTuff("-100I8")` → `-100`. ✅

The implementation already supported signed integers (`I` prefix with two's complement bounds checking), so no changes to `index.ts` were needed — just adding the test case was sufficient.
